task: Tenants need per-tenant settings without duplicating the whole base configuration. Apply tenant overrides on top of the base config in tenant_config.py.
known_issue: dict(self.base_config) is a shallow copy: config[section] is the same nested dict object as base_config[section], so .update(values) writes one tenant's overrides into the shared base config. Every tenant served afterwards silently inherits earlier tenants' overrides — cross-tenant configuration bleed.
expected_correct_fix: Deep-copy before mutating: config = copy.deepcopy(self.base_config), or copy each section (config = {k: dict(v) for k, v in self.base_config.items()}) before updating.
